title: [ExportVerilog] Concurrent Assertion Messages Should Use $sampled (maybe)
body: Currently, we emit concurrent assertions with format strings like: ```verilog assert_1: assert property (@(posedge clock) foo) else $error("hello world: %d", foo); ``` However, this is ill-advised by the System Verilog 2017 specification (Section 16.9.3). The advised way is to use `$sampled`: ```verilog assert_2: assert property (@(posedge clock) foo) else $error("hello world: %d", $sampled(foo)); ``` However, either choice here hits different VCS lint warnings which can be seen in this [EDA Playground sketch](https://www.edaplayground.com/x/c7my). We either get without using `$sampled`: ``` Lint-[SVA-NSVU] Non-sampled variable used testbench.sv, 15 "foo" Non-sampled variable "foo" is used in action block of assertion. ``` Or with using `$sampled`: ``` Lint-[SVA-AECASR] Assertion evaluation can affect simulation results testbench.sv, 16 "$sampled(foo)" The assertion contains function call "$sampled(foo)" which can have side effects. Shutting of this assertion may cause change in simulation behavior. ``` Ideally we pick the SV 2017-advised option of `$sampled`, but also figure out how to emit lint waivers (either inline or in a file) that enable this to not generate warnings (or errors if one of these has been promoted to such by a build system). h/t @drom for helping figure out the SV-advised approach.
